title: artemisia commutata var. richardsoniana
intro: Artemisia commutata var. richardsoniana, also known as Balsam Sage or Richardson's Sage, is a perennial herb native to Western Canada and the northwestern United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe and protect the skin, as well as its antimicrobial and antifungal properties that aid in wound healing. The health benefits of Balsam Sage include reducing inflammation and promoting skin regeneration, making it effective in treating minor cuts and scrapes, and it has also been used to alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Balsam Sage can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and can also be used in topical applications to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
